content.body: Hi all, I'm co-chair of the Private Advertising Technology Working Group. We have a spec that we'd like the group's expertise and review on. I've opened an issue in what is hopefully the right space. Please let me know if I need to file it anywhere else or if there's any more information needed - https://github.com/tc39/agendas/issues/2126
AramZS: I'm not sure what TC39 reviewing this is supposed to look at?
content.formatted_body: AramZS: I'm not sure what TC39 reviewing this is supposed to look at?

content.body: Yes, it would be helpful to know what you are looking for specifically from TC39 folks.
you may be looking for W3C TAG review: https://tag.w3.org/
I don't think TC39 would have any feedback for something like this
agreed. although my personal feedback is, i'm not interested in anything that makes it easier or cheaper for advertisers to show me ads
that sounds like the opposite of what would be good for web users

I think the hope is any feedback on internals or APIs and how the function within the larger ecosystem TC39 works on. The W3C review checklist suggests that for proposals that introduce new JS APIs we work with TC39. So any feedback is appreciated and also if there's no interest, we'd miss having your voice in the process, but I know we all have limited time

Our position stems from the idea that this is a behavior that is already happening, we want to make it so that behavior goes away from invasive surveillance approaches and toward privacy safe tools with clear flows and user capability to opt out. One of the editors has a good interview on the topic here - https://monopoly-report.com/p/the-w3c-attribution-api

Why I'm involved is: It's better for users to give them better privacy, better control and better transparency into a thing that is already happening to them while securing methods that keep the web open and give publishers options other than closing down paywalls and blocking users from accessing well-reported information (my bias on the table - I work for a journalism outlet).

This is my first direct involvement over here at TC39 so I'm here on the suggestion of the more experienced haha. Please let me know if it would be helpful to add more details somewhere. I can point to specific areas of the spec if that would help.

Given that no part of the proposal impacts language level APIs, syntax, or (from what I can see) global scope, I don't believe there's really anything here for the committee to take a position on. This sits at a higher level (web apis, and specifically browsers).
We can and IMO should have opinions about API design on the web
more people with API design sense reviewing e.g. WebCrypto could have saved a lot of developers a lot of headaches

I agree it would probably be reasonable for the committee to eventually publish a technical report on general guidance around higher level apis, but it's not likely worth our time reviewing individual specs.
probably not worth time in plenary or from the committee as a whole but individual delegates can comment
(as long as they don't purport to speak for the committee as a whole)

generally, I expect we'd be more interested where a proposal potentially impacts ECMAScript itself or introduces a broadly reusable programming abstraction or general API (e.g. web crypto). what they have is very domain specific. so I don’t see a particular TC39 review item here, unless there is a specific ECMAScript interaction they'd like us to consider
